description: Disulfiram + active-metabolite plasma PK parameters. Anchored to Lee 2018 (population PK in HIV trial, 500-2000 mg dosing), Johansson 1989 (single-dose 400 mg + Me-DTC kinetics + DER dose-titration in volunteers), Palatty 2011 (125 mg/day clinical use), and Faiman 1989 (DER acetaldehyde threshold).
ba_source: Lee 2018 PMC6379104 — '80-95% absorbed from GI tract'; midpoint 87.5% used as point estimate
clearance_source: Lee 2018 PMC6379104 — DSF apparent CL/F = 0.53 L/hr, IIV 36%. Note: This is total clearance to all 4 measured metabolites; reflects rapid first-pass and reductive metabolism.
vd_source: Lee 2018 — central compartment Vd ~1.3 L; widely distributed to kidney, pancreas, liver, GI per same paper text. NOTE: 1.3 L is fitting-compartment Vd, not true body Vd which is much larger; treat as scaling parameter for AUC, not as physiological volume.
absorption_source: Lee 2018 — ka = 0.08 hr^-1 (slow absorption, reflects micronization-limited dissolution and food effect).
half_life_source: Lee 2018 — estimated DSF t1/2 ~7 hr; >90% eliminated within 72 hr.
nonlinearity_note: Lee 2018 — supra-proportional AUC increase above 1000 mg attributed to CYP2E1/3A4 saturation. Below 1000 mg dose, kinetics are approximately linear.
metabolite_active_for_GSDMD: carbamathione + DETC-MeSO (DSF itself + reduced metabolite DDTC are the active GSDMD modifiers per Hu 2020)
metabolite_active_for_DER: DDTC-Me (Me-DTC); per Faiman 1989 — most-potent ALDH inhibitor among DSF / DDTC / DDTC-Me, and the metabolite that scales DER severity
metabolite_separation_note: GSDMD-relevant covalent activity comes primarily from DSF + reduced metabolites that retain the thiuram disulfide / dithiocarbamate motif (Hu 2020). DER comes primarily from Me-DTC (Yourick/Faiman 1989). The two activities are NOT fully separable at the metabolite level — DSF dose → DDTC → splits into reductive vs methylation/CYP3A4 paths. But the DOSE → MAX-EFFECT curves saturate at different concentrations, which is what creates the potential dose window.

single_dose_PK.Cmax_method: Cmax ≈ F·D / (Vd·(1 + k_el/ka)) approximation, then refined with reported AUC scaling; uses CL=0.53 L/hr, Vd=1.3 L, ka=0.08 hr^-1, F=0.875. AT THIS LOW DOSE, kinetics fully linear.
single_dose_PK.comment: 125 mg dose is clinically used (Palatty 2011 — 51 patients × 60 days, well-tolerated). Lee 2018 PK model extrapolated downward.
Standard AUD daily maintenance dose. Linear extrapolation from Lee 2018.
Lee 2018 anchor dose. Observed AUC_72h_summed = 3420 mg·hr/L across 5 analytes. AUC of parent DSF alone is much lower.
Lee 2018 anchor dose. Supra-proportional AUC due to CYP saturation; Cmax estimate widened to reflect IIV.

active_metabolite_DDTC_Me_PK.name: diethyldithiocarbamate methyl ester (Me-DTC, DDTC-Me)
active_metabolite_DDTC_Me_PK.role: Primary alcohol-deterrent metabolite (Faiman 1989). Methylation of DDTC; CYP-bioactivated to MeSO further.
active_metabolite_DDTC_Me_PK.dose_scaling: Mean steady-state plasma Me-DTC is proportional to oral DSF dose across 100-300 mg in volunteers per Johansson 1989; assume linear up to ~500 mg, then sub-linear above 1000 mg due to CYP saturation.
